meta.note: Indonesia + Vietnam + Cuba sweep, published books. Deduped against existing records (all new). Indonesia New Order AGO bans (HRW 1998 report, with decree context) + 2019 military book-raid seizures; AGO banning power was struck down by the Constitutional Court in 2010, so these are historical. Held: Vietnamese poems/journal pieces and a confiscated diary manuscript (periodical/unpublished), plus two weakly-sourced de-facto bans (Đêm giữa ban ngày, Un Excommunié); Cuba's author-level Ponte case. Cuba suppression is often via withdrawal/non-publication rather than formal decree.

entries.title: Memoar Oei Tjoe Tat
entries.description_ban: Memoir of a Sukarno-era minister (edited by Pramoedya Ananta Toer and Stanley Adi Prasetyo); banned by the Attorney General's Office on 25 September 1995 for contradicting the New Order account of the 1965 events.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific AGO ban; documented by Human Rights Watch.

entries.title: The Mute's Soliloquy
entries.description_ban: Pramoedya's memoir (Nyanyi Sunyi Seorang Bisu) of his 14-year Buru Island imprisonment; banned as the work of a former Lekra figure deemed a communist sympathiser.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific AGO ban; documented by Human Rights Watch.

entries.title: Sang Pemula
entries.description_ban: Political biography of proto-nationalist Tirto Adhi Suriyo; among the Pramoedya works banned by the Attorney General under the New Order.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific AGO ban; documented by Human Rights Watch (medium confidence).

entries.title: Di Bawah Lentera Merah
entries.description_ban: Study of the early nationalist movement (Sarekat Islam Semarang); banned under the New Order for treating leftist organising of the 1910s–20s.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific AGO ban; documented by Human Rights Watch (medium confidence).

entries.title: Tan Malaka: Pergulatan Menuju Republik
entries.description_ban: Indonesian translation of Poeze's biography of communist leader Tan Malaka; banned because it 'could result in the spread of Marxist-Leninist teachings'.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific AGO ban; documented by Human Rights Watch.

entries.title: Bayang-Bayang PKI
entries.description_ban: Documentary book on the 1965 events; banned by the Attorney General's Office on 22 April 1996 for allegedly 'inverting or obscuring facts' about the coup.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific AGO ban; documented by Human Rights Watch.

entries.title: Primadosa
entries.description_ban: Polemic alleging Suharto was behind the 1965 coup; banned for attacking the regime's legitimacy.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific AGO ban; documented by Human Rights Watch.

entries.title: Kehormatan Bagi Yang Berhak
entries.description_ban: Argues Sukarno was not involved in the 1965 coup; banned for contradicting official history.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific AGO ban; documented by Human Rights Watch.

entries.title: The Devious Dalang: Sukarno and the So-Called Untung Putsch
entries.description_ban: Transcripts on Sukarno and the 1965 putsch; banned in 1990 as liable to 'disturb public order'.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific AGO ban; documented by Human Rights Watch (medium confidence).

entries.title: Permesta: Kandasnya Sebuah Cita-Cita
entries.description_ban: History of the 1950s Permesta rebellion; banned for conflicting with the official armed-forces account.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific AGO ban; documented by Human Rights Watch (medium confidence).

entries.title: Kronik '65
entries.description_ban: Documentary chronicle of the 1965 events; seized by the Padang military command in January 2019 as alleged 'communist propaganda' (the AGO's formal banning power had been struck down in 2010).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific military seizure; documented by The Jakarta Post.

entries.title: Mengincar Bung Besar
entries.description_ban: Account of seven assassination attempts on Sukarno; among the titles seized in the January 2019 Padang military book raids.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific military seizure; documented by The Jakarta Post.

entries.title: The Crystal Messenger
entries.description_ban: Phạm Thị Hoài's first novel (Thiên sứ), published in Hanoi in 1988 and later banned by the government over its critical, taboo-breaking view of contemporary Vietnam. Won the Frankfurt LiBeraturpreis.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented by Index on Censorship.

entries.title: Beyond Illusions
entries.description_ban: Bestseller (Bên kia bờ ảo vọng) depicting disillusionment with Communist Party corruption; banned after the author's 1991 arrest, when her entire oeuvre was prohibited in Vietnam.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented by Publishers Weekly.

entries.title: Memories of a Pure Spring
entries.description_ban: Novel of a composer and his wife in post-war Vietnam; never published in Vietnam after the author's 1991 arrest and internal exile, her work banned.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented via Wikipedia (medium confidence).

entries.title: No Man's Land
entries.description_ban: Tragic post-1975 love story (Chốn vắng) banned in Vietnam and published abroad; won the Grand Prix des Lectrices de Elle in 2007.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented by the Global Literature in Libraries Initiative.

entries.title: Về Kinh Bắc
entries.description_ban: Poetry collection (composed 1959–60); in 1982 the handwritten manuscript was seized en route to Saigon and Hoàng Cầm was jailed at Hỏa Lò over it. Later rehabilitated (State Prize 2007).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific seizure/jailing; documented by Văn Việt (medium confidence).

entries.title: Sát thủ đầu mưng mủ
entries.description_ban: Illustrated book of contemporary slang idioms; the Arts Publishing House ordered it recalled for 'harming the purity of Vietnamese'. Reissued in 2013 under a new title with revisions.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific recall; documented by Tuổi Trẻ.

entries.title: El mundo alucinante
entries.description_ban: Won a UNEAC mention but was refused publication in Cuba and banned for its anti-revolutionary tone; the manuscript was smuggled out and published abroad.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific suppression; documented via Wikipedia (Reinaldo Arenas).

entries.title: Otra vez el mar
entries.description_ban: Manuscript repeatedly confiscated and destroyed by Cuban authorities and rewritten; never publishable in Cuba, smuggled out and published in Spain.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific suppression; documented via Wikipedia (Reinaldo Arenas).

entries.title: Paradiso
entries.description_ban: Officials classified the novel as pornography for its homoerotic eighth chapter; after the 1971 Padilla affair Lezama's books were removed from circulation and he was barred from publishing.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific withdrawal; documented by Literary Hub and PBS VOCES.

entries.title: En mi jardín pastan los héroes
entries.description_ban: The novel Cuban police searched for but never found; smuggled out as letters and published in exile. Distinct from the already-held Fuera del juego.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific pre-publication suppression; documented in a Padilla obituary (Latin American Studies) and the Padilla-affair record.

entries.title: Contra toda esperanza
entries.description_ban: Prison memoir of 22 years in Castro's jails; Cuba mounted a defamation campaign against Valladares and the book never circulated on the island.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific de-facto ban; documented via Wikipedia (Armando Valladares) (medium confidence).

entries.title: La nada cotidiana
entries.description_ban: Highly critical of the regime; after its French publication Cuba forbade the author's return (she was exiled) and the book was suppressed on the island.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific suppression; documented via Wikipedia (Zoé Valdés) (medium confidence).

entries.title: Trilogía sucia de La Habana
entries.description_ban: Banned in Cuba for its explicit sex and bleak portrait of 'Special Period' Havana, while published in some twenty countries.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented via Wikipedia (Pedro Juan Gutiérrez) (medium confidence).

entries.title: Todos se van
entries.description_ban: Won Spain's Bruguera Prize, but the author's work is not published or distributed in Cuba; widely described as banned at home.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific de-facto ban; documented by Havana Times (medium confidence).
